findings.evidence: `quotientPointsHom` is just `(mapPointsFunctor (mkQuotient H I)).app A` at lines 56-60; the proof at lines 84-89 only extensionalizes along quotient representatives. The generic precomposition API already exists at `CommHopfAlgCat.lean:71-82` and `HopfMap.lean:65-83`, but `rg "mapPointsFunctor.*injective|Injective.*mapPointsFunctor|mapDomain.*Surjective"` finds no general injectivity lemma.
findings.fix: First add a general lemma such as `mapPointsFunctor_app_injective_of_surjective {H K} (φ : H ⟶ K) (hφ : Function.Surjective φ.hom) (A) : Function.Injective ((mapPointsFunctor φ).app A)`, then derive this quotient lemma from `Ideal.Quotient.mkₐ_surjective`.
findings.issue: `quotientPointsHom_injective` is a special-case proof for Hopf-ideal quotients, but it only uses that precomposition is by a surjective coordinate morphism.

summary: The quotient point API mostly sits at the intended closed-subgroup level, but one theorem is proved at an unnecessarily special level instead of the natural functor-of-points level.
